hcd_data.architectural_style: House-form, Semi-detached, Bay-and-Gable (confirmed – HCD Plan Vol. 2)
hcd_data.statement_of_contribution: Each property has value as a nineteenth-century House-form building that reflects vernacular interpretations of Victorian architectural styles. Through its construction date and typology, each property is directly associated with the District’s early residential development from the 1850s to the early 1900s. Constructed as a semi-detached pair, the properties retain most of their original building features including mirrored facades, cross-gabled roofline, first storey bay windows, brick cladding with polychromatic brick detailing, and fenestration pattern with round and segmentally arched openings. Contextually, each property shares a consistent setback, scale, and massing with the other contributing properties on the street. Each property supports and maintains the residential character of the streetscape and is functionally, visually, and historically linked to the District.

context.street_character: One-way | Residential street | Tree-lined | Heritage character

facade_detail.composition: The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 3 storeys and is capped by a cross-gable, consistent with its semi-detached bay-and-gable house-form typology.
facade_detail.opening_rhythm: The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 2, 2, 1 window bays from lower to upper levels, using double-hung sash windows, and 1 principal entrance.
facade_detail.heritage_expression: Character-defining facade elements include cornice, decorative brick, original windows, bay window.
facade_detail.heritage_summary: Constructed as a semi-detached pair, the properties retain most of their original building features including mirrored facades, cross-gabled roofline, first storey bay windows, brick cladding with polychromatic brick detailing, and fenestration pattern with round and segmentally arched openings. Contextually, each property shares a consistent setback, scale, and massing with the other contributing properties on the street.

facade_description: Semi-detached three-storey bay-and-gable in brick under a cross-gable roof. Features include a double-height three-sided projecting bay, decorative bargeboards, a decorative cornice and polychromatic brickwork. Constructed 1890-1903.
heritage_summary: Constructed as a semi-detached pair, the properties retain most of their original building features including mirrored facades, cross-gabled roofline, first storey bay windows, brick cladding with polychromatic brick detailing, and fenestration pattern with round and segmentally arched openings. Contextually, each property shares a consistent setback, scale, and massing with the other contributing properties on the street.
